description_long: This object consists of two rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, rectangular base. The panels are dark, likely made of photovoltaic cells, and are positioned side by side with a slight gap between them. The panels are tilted upwards at an angle, supported by a visible mounting structure that appears to be metallic. The base is light-colored and flat, providing stability and possibly housing electrical components beneath the panels. The overall design is compact and suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small ground installations. The structure is utilitarian, with no visible cabling or inverter components on the exterior.
description: A pair of dark, tilted solar panels mounted on a flat, light-colored base with a metallic support structure. The panels are side by side and slightly elevated for optimal sunlight exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two dark solar panels side by side on a flat base, with their tilted surfaces clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the panels and the supporting structure beneath them.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back edges of the panels and the flat base, with the tilt less pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the elevation and separation between the two panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2 panels, side by side
